mnemonics: FLiD Seeks the Maximum Line (Fisher's Linear Discriminant Seeks the Maximum Separation Line)
keyPoints: Goal: Project data onto a line to maximize separation between classes and minimize variance within each class.
The solution is found by maximizing the ratio of between-class variance to within-class variance.
It is a generative method that assumes data from each class is normally distributed.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis) for classification; FLD is the dimension reduction technique that LDA is based on.
Forgetting the underlying assumption of normally distributed classes with equal covariance matrices.
Applying it to problems with more than two classes without using the multi-class generalization.
